title: Contains Duplicate
description:
Given an integer array `nums`, return `true` if any value appears **at least twice** in the array, and return `false` if every element is distinct.

**Constraints:**
- 1 <= nums.length <= 10^5
- -10^9 <= nums[i] <= 10^9

examples.explanation: The value 1 appears at index 0 and index 3.
Multiple values appear more than once.

editorial: Use a hash set. Iterate through the array. For each number, check if it's already in the set — if yes, return true (duplicate found). Otherwise, add it to the set. If you finish the loop without finding a duplicate, return false. Time: O(n), Space: O(n). Alternative: sort the array and check adjacent elements — O(n log n) time, O(1) extra space.

rexHints.level1: You need to remember which numbers you've already seen as you scan through the array.
rexHints.level2: A hash set gives O(1) lookup to check if a number was seen before.
rexHints.level3: Create an empty Set. For each num in nums: if set.has(num) return true, else set.add(num). Return false after the loop.
